commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `/new` and `/reset` reset-command matching case-insensitive across reset parsing, reset hook emission, and startup action selection, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +4. Total +5 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the lower-level handler: current main uses case-sensitive reset/new regexes when commandBodyNormalized is not already canonicalized. I did not run tests because this read-only review forbids commands that may create artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should wait for contributor-supplied real behavior proof; there is no narrow code repair task for automation to perform.

target.title: fix: match session reset commands case-insensitively
target.body:
Summary
- Match `/new` and `/reset` command verbs case-insensitively across reset parsing and reset-hook emission paths.
- Normalize the parsed reset action before dispatch so mixed-case commands keep the same behavior as lowercase commands.
- Add regression coverage for `/NEW`, `/RESET`, and `/Reset <reason>`.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-mode.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-hooks.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: uppercase and mixed-case `/new` and `/reset` commands are handled as commands instead of falling through as normal user text.
Real environment tested: local upstream/main-based worktree under `.claude/worktrees/fix-session-command-case-insensitive`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-mode.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-hooks.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: tests cover uppercase reset/new hooks and mixed-case reset with a reason tail.
Observed result after fix: Vitest passed with 2 test files and 14 tests; diff whitespace check was clean.
What was not tested: no live channel command roundtrip was performed.
target.author: lanzhi-lee
